pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 62-68: Forest class assigned value 1 in 4 distinct corners; task.json description: 1=Forest
task.json description line 6: 'This raster contains 3 pixel values: 1=Forest, 2=Water, 3=Urban.'

setup_task.sh lines 60-68: Forest (value 1) assigned to 4 distinct 20x20 pixel regions in corners (Top-Left, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, Bottom-Right), each separated by Water and Urban classes, resulting in 4 distinct polygons when rasterized

privileged_info_summary: The raster is synthetically generated with dimensions 100x100 pixels in WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N (EPSG:32610). It contains 3 classes: Forest (class 1, 16% of pixels), Water (class 2, 80%), and Urban (class 3, 4%). The Forest class consists of 4 separate 20x20 pixel regions located in the four corners of the raster, ensuring 4 distinct polygons when polygonized. When filtered to extract only Forest features (class value 1), the output should contain exactly 4 polygon features.
